title: I Call You to March with Pride, to March toward Victory!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly election will come in May 2016. I know this, and so do the sentinels of our Kazhagam. We are preparing to face that election and labour for the victory of our Dravidian movement. Meanwhile, driven by the petty desire to bring this movement down, the ruling party here, the AIADMK, fabricates false reports and uses the influence it has acquired at the all-India level to seek strength, inviting people to behold the splendour that such strength can bring. It has forced a doubt into the minds of the people: is there a government at all in this country, particularly in our State? Having piled up crores upon crores of rupees, its members boast, “We shall buy this victory.” They beat their chests as though they can turn even one or two courts of justice into courts of corruption. Against the arrogance of these power-intoxicated dreamers, I see our beloved Kazhagam cadres standing in the field with the noble aim that the principle of democracy must triumph. Every day, I rejoice to see the fervour with which they hurry to me to place in my hands whatever money or coins they have been able to gather.

Udanpirappē, you and brethren like you toil and toil to earn modest sums. Yet with such enthusiasm and concern, and with the firm conviction that “this movement must win; this alone is the star of hope for the future growth of the Dravidian movement,” you place those sums in our hands. They will be spent properly. The light of “DMK victory,” equal to the rising of a crore suns, will stream into our hearts; our opponents will be bewildered at the sight, while our kith, kin, and townspeople rejoice. The day when I shall delight in seeing the radiant faces of brethren like you in the bright light of that rising sun is only a few months away.

After that, there will be no need to sigh, “This country lies in a dark chamber.” Here is a future filled with light. Through the DMK Government that will govern Tamil Nadu, and through the glory of our two-coloured flag, you, I, and the lakhs of Dravidian brethren who stand with us are going to witness a time that will bring joy to the hearts of crores of Tamils spread across the world. There is no need to regard it as a dream. This conviction is one that will give us, and the Kazhagam, ever greater nourishment and strength.

There is not the slightest doubt that we shall behold a flourishing Dravidian land beneath the shade of this movement's flag, a flag nurtured by the blood shed by many Dravidian martyrs. With that faith:

The day when we recognise the dignity of democracy, uphold and nurture its purity with the conviction that it alone is the finest support for this soil, and ensure that its benefits reach even the poor and humble at the farthest edge, that alone will be a day of joy for you, for me, and for brethren like us. March towards that day. To raise the cry of victory there, thrust aside the hostility surrounding us and come! I call upon you to march proudly in this democratic battle, along the path of righteousness, the path of peace, Anna's path, and Periyar's path.

Day after day, you look towards the direction where I am and bring the coins you have knotted away in your hand as the Kazhagam's election fund. May that pure service help our Kazhagam grow each day and gain still greater strength.
